input.plan:
# Task #204 — Implement `Highlight` agnostic core

## Context

Implement the framework-agnostic `Highlight` utility component
(`spec/components/utility/highlight.md`) — a stateless utility that splits a
text string into highlighted / non-highlighted chunks based on one or more
search queries, with three match strategies (`Contains`, `StartsWith`,
`Fuzzy`), Unicode-aware case folding via ICU4X `CaseMapper`, and multi-query
overlap merging.

The component is consumed by adapters (Leptos, Dioxus) that render each
returned chunk as `<mark>` or `<span>`. Agnostic core is the only deliverable
here — adapter wrappers are out of scope.

GitHub issue: <https://github.com/anthropics/ars-ui/issues/204> · 3 points ·
no dependencies.

## Files to create / modify

| Action | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Create | `crates/ars-components/src/utility/highlight.rs` | Component module: `Props`, `MatchStrategy`, `HighlightChunk`, `highlight_chunks`, `Part`, `Api`, tests. |
| Modify | `crates/ars-components/src/utility/mod.rs` | Add `pub mod highlight;` entry, alphabetized under existing utility modules. |
| Create | `crates/ars-components/src/utility/snapshots/ars_components__utility__highlight__tests__*.snap` | Insta snapshots — generated via `cargo insta review` and committed (label `snapshot-reviewed` on PR). |

No `Cargo.toml` changes (no new dependencies — `ars-i18n` already pulled in by
`ars-components` and exposes locale-aware case mapping via
`ars_i18n::to_lowercase`).

## Public API (from spec §1)

```rust
// spec/components/utility/highlight.md lines 20–46
pub struct Props {
    pub query: Vec<String>,
    pub text: String,
    pub ignore_case: bool,
    pub match_strategy: MatchStrategy,
}

pub enum MatchStrategy {
    Contains,
    StartsWith,
    Fuzzy,
}

pub struct HighlightChunk<'a> {
    pub text: &'a str,
    pub highlighted: bool,
}

pub fn highlight_chunks<'a>(props: &'a Props, locale: &Locale) -> Vec<HighlightChunk<'a>>;
```

Defaults (per spec text):

- `ignore_case: bool` defaults to `true`.
- `match_strategy: MatchStrategy` defaults to `MatchStrategy::Contains`
  (most permissive — pure inclusion match, matches Ark UI default).
- `query` and `text` default to empty vec / empty string.

## Utility-pattern conformance additions

The Highlight spec §1.2 says it uses a "function-based API" instead of the
standard `ConnectApi` / `AttrMap` pattern — but the issue's acceptance
criteria still require "snapshot tests cover the component's `connect()` /
`Api` `AttrMap` output for every anatomy part", and the spec template
(`foundation/10-component-spec-template.md` §4.1.2) mandates `Props` / `Part`
/ `Api` for all stateless components. We satisfy both by adding the standard
utility shape **alongside** the function:

```rust
#[derive(ComponentPart)]
#[scope = "highlight"]
pub enum Part {
    Root,
}

pub struct Api { props: Props }

impl Api {
    pub const fn new(props: Props) -> Self;
    pub const fn props(&self) -> &Props;
    pub fn query(&self) -> &[String];
    pub fn text(&self) -> &str;
    pub const fn ignore_case(&self) -> bool;
    pub const fn match_strategy(&self) -> MatchStrategy;
    pub fn root_attrs(&self) -> AttrMap;                       // Part::Root
    pub fn chunk_attrs(&self, highlighted: bool) -> AttrMap;   // Chunk (parametric)
    pub fn chunks<'a>(&'a self, locale: &Locale) -> Vec<HighlightChunk<'a>>;
}

impl ConnectApi for Api {
    type Part = Part;
    fn part_attrs(&self, part: Part) -> AttrMap { /* Root → root_attrs */ }
}
```

`Part::Chunk` is intentionally not a Part variant: the chunk anatomy slot is
parametric on a runtime boolean (`highlighted`), and `ConnectApi::part_attrs`
takes a `Part` by value with no payload. Adapters call `chunk_attrs(bool)`
per emitted chunk. Doc-comment on `Part` explains this.

`root_attrs()` emits `data-ars-scope="highlight"` + `data-ars-part="root"`
(via `Part::Root.data_attrs()`). `chunk_attrs(b)` emits
`data-ars-part="highlight-chunk"` + `data-ars-highlighted="true"|"false"`.

## Algorithm — `highlight_chunks`

Reuse `ars_i18n::to_lowercase(text, locale)` for Unicode-aware,
locale-sensitive normalisation. This is the **same** helper the
already-implemented `ars-collections::typeahead` (`typeahead.rs:255,297`) uses
for case-insensitive matching, so Turkic locales (`İ`/`i`/`I`/`ı`), German
eszett, Greek final sigma, etc. are already handled by the underlying ICU4X
`CaseMapper`. See "Open question — case folding" below.

Pipeline:

1. **Short-circuit empty input.** If `props.query.is_empty()` or every entry
   is empty, return `vec![HighlightChunk { text: &props.text, highlighted: false }]`.
   If `props.text.is_empty()`, return an empty `Vec`.
2. **Build normalised text + byte-mapping** (only when `ignore_case`):
   - Iterate `props.text.char_indices()`. For each char, take its substring
     (`text[orig_start..orig_end]`), pass to `ars_i18n::to_lowercase`, and
     append the lowercased bytes to a `String` while pushing `orig_start`
     into a parallel `Vec<usize>` once per appended byte. Push a sentinel
     `text.len()` at the end of the map.
   - The resulting `byte_map[i]` ≡ original-text byte index of the source
     char that produced normalised byte `i`. Property:
     `byte_map[match_end_in_normalised]` is always an original-char boundary
     (or `text.len()` at the end), because each fold block is emitted
     contiguously.
   - When `!ignore_case`, the normalised text **is** the original text and
     `byte_map` is identity (`0..=text.len()`).
3. **Collect raw match ranges per query × strategy:**
   - `Contains`: `normalised_text.match_indices(&normalised_query)`. Map each
     `(start, _)` to original range `(byte_map[start], byte_map[start + normalised_query.len()])`.
   - `StartsWith`: if `normalised_text.starts_with(&normalised_query)`, emit
     a single range `(0, byte_map[normalised_query.len()])`.
   - `Fuzzy`: iterate normalised text char-by-char, advancing a query-char
     cursor; each matched character emits one original-char range
     `(orig_start, orig_end)`. Use `char_indices()` on the **original** text
     and `to_lowercase` per-char to avoid the byte-map indirection for the
     1-char case (cleaner + handles Unicode expansion correctly).
4. **Merge overlapping / adjacent ranges.** Sort by start; sweep merging
   `(a, b), (c, d)` where `c <= b` into `(a, max(b, d))`. Adjacency-merge
   (`c == b`) keeps the rendered output minimal — naturally consolidates
   fuzzy 1-char hits that happen to land next to each other and meets spec
   §3.1's "consolidate adjacent highlighted chunks" recommendation.
5. **Build chunks.** Walk the merged ranges; emit a non-highlighted
   `HighlightChunk` for each gap and a highlighted one for each range. Skip
   zero-length segments so we never emit an empty chunk.

### Edge cases the algorithm must hit

- Empty query / all-empty queries → one non-highlighted chunk (`text`).
- Empty text → empty vec.
- Query longer than text → no matches → one non-highlighted chunk.
- All-matched text → one highlighted chunk.
- Query at end of text → highlighted tail + no trailing non-highlighted chunk.
- Multi-query overlapping ranges merged into one chunk.
- Fuzzy with `query.chars().count() > text.chars().count()` → no matches
  → one non-highlighted chunk.
- `ignore_case = false` → byte-exact matching, no normalisation pass.

## Tests (TDD — written before implementation)

All inline in `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { ... }` per `separator.rs` precedent.
Snapshot tests use `insta::assert_snapshot!` and live under
`crates/ars-components/src/utility/snapshots/`.

### Strategy / props matrix (named tests required by issue)

| # | Test name | Coverage |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `contains_strategy_matches_substring` | Single contains match in middle of text → 3 chunks. |
| 2 | `contains_strategy_finds_all_occurrences` | Repeated query → N+1 alternating chunks. |
| 3 | `starts_with_strategy_matches_at_start` | Match only at position 0. |
| 4 | `starts_with_strategy_no_match_in_middle` | Query is in middle of text → single non-highlighted chunk. |
| 5 | `fuzzy_strategy_matches_in_order_with_gaps` | `"abc"` in `"a_b_c"` → 5 chunks (3 highlighted, 2 gaps). |
| 6 | `fuzzy_strategy_no_match_when_chars_missing` | Missing char in query → no fuzzy match. |
| 7 | `unicode_case_folding_turkic_dotted_i` | `tr` locale: `"İ"` query matches `"i"` text and vice versa. |
| 8 | `unicode_case_folding_german_eszett` | `de` locale: `"ss"` query matches `"ß"` text when `ignore_case`. |
| 9 | `multi_query_overlapping_ranges_merged` | Two queries `["foo", "oob"]` on `"foobar"` → merged into single highlighted span `"foob"`. |
| 10 | `empty_query_returns_single_non_highlighted_chunk` | `query: vec![]` → `[Chunk { text, highlighted: false }]`. |
| 11 | `all_empty_queries_returns_single_non_highlighted_chunk` | `query: vec!["".into(), "".into()]` → same. |
| 12 | `ignore_case_true_matches_case_insensitive` | `"hello"` query matches `"HELLO"` text with `ignore_case = true`. |
| 13 | `ignore_case_false_matches_case_sensitive` | Same input with `ignore_case = false` → no match. |
| 14 | `empty_text_returns_empty_vec` | Defensive edge case. |
| 15 | `query_longer_than_text_returns_single_non_highlighted_chunk` | Defensive edge case. |

### Props / `Api` ergonomic tests (mirror separator.rs §"Props" and §"Api accessors")

| # | Test name |
|---|---|
| 16 | `props_default_is_contains_ignore_case_true` |
| 17 | `props_clone_and_partial_eq_round_trip` |
| 18 | `props_debug_impl_non_empty` |
| 19 | `api_exposes_all_props_fields` |
| 20 | `api_props_round_trips` |
| 21 | `part_attrs_root_equals_root_attrs` |

### Snapshot tests (insta)

| # | Test name | Snapshots `connect()` / chunk output |
|---|---|---|
| 22 | `highlight_root_snapshot` | `root_attrs()` AttrMap. |
| 23 | `highlight_chunk_highlighted_snapshot` | `chunk_attrs(true)`. |
| 24 | `highlight_chunk_not_highlighted_snapshot` | `chunk_attrs(false)`. |
| 25 | `highlight_chunks_contains_en_snapshot` | `highlight_chunks` output for canonical Contains case. |
| 26 | `highlight_chunks_starts_with_snapshot` | Starts-with case. |
| 27 | `highlight_chunks_fuzzy_snapshot` | Fuzzy case. |
| 28 | `highlight_chunks_multi_query_merged_snapshot` | Two overlapping queries. |
| 29 | `highlight_chunks_turkic_snapshot` | Turkic case folding. |
| 30 | `highlight_chunks_german_eszett_snapshot` | German eszett. |

PR must apply the `snapshot-reviewed` label after committing the snapshots
(per `CLAUDE.md` "Development Workflow" item 10).

## Key existing utilities to reuse

| Reusable | Where | How |
|---|---|---|
| `ars_i18n::to_lowercase(text, locale)` | `crates/ars-i18n/src/case.rs:70` | Locale-aware lowercase using ICU4X `CaseMapper`; matches `typeahead.rs:255` precedent. |
| `ars_i18n::Locale` | `crates/ars-i18n/src/locale.rs` | Function parameter. |
| `ars_core::{AttrMap, ComponentPart, ConnectApi, HasId, HtmlAttr}` | `crates/ars-core/src/lib.rs` | Standard utility-component scaffolding (same imports as `separator.rs:12`). |
| Insta snapshot pattern | `crates/ars-components/src/utility/separator.rs:418–444` | `snapshot_attrs` helper + `assert_snapshot!`. |

## Spec conformance notes

The Highlight spec has two small inconsistencies worth flagging — both
should be **fixed in this PR** alongside the implementation per `CLAUDE.md`
"Spec/code mismatch resolution":

1. **`spec/components/utility/highlight.md:65`** — references
   `CaseMapper::fold_turkic()` / `CaseMapper::fold()`. We use
   `ars_i18n::to_lowercase` (which wraps `CaseMapper::lowercase_to_string`,
   handles Turkic via `language_identifier`). The acceptance criterion
   "Unicode case folding uses ICU4X CaseMapper, not ASCII-only lowering" is
   satisfied — the comment in the spec should be reworded to describe the
   helper used (or the helper renamed). Proposed edit: replace the explicit
   ICU4X method names with "delegate to the locale-aware case helpers in
   `ars-i18n`, which use ICU4X `CaseMapper` under the hood."
2. **`spec/components/utility/highlight.md:111`** — the adapter example
   calls `highlight_chunks(&props)` (one arg). Function signature is
   `(&props, &locale)`. Update the example to pass `&locale`.

Both are doc-only edits in the same component spec file. No other spec files
touch this component.

## Verification (end-to-end)

After implementation, run **in this order**:

```bash
# 1. Focused tests for the new module
cargo test -p ars-components --lib utility::highlight

# 2. Review and accept snapshots, then re-run (must remain green)
cargo insta review
cargo test -p ars-components --lib utility::highlight

# 3. Module-level lints / docs
cargo clippy -p ars-components --all-features -- -D warnings
cargo doc -p ars-components --no-deps

# 4. Coverage spot-check on the new module
cargo llvm-cov test -p ars-components --text -- utility::highlight

# 5. Spec validator picks up frontmatter / manifest consistency
cargo run -p spec-tool -- validate

# 6. Full pre-PR gate (after user approval)
cargo xci
```

PR body MUST include `Closes #204`; PR MUST get the `snapshot-reviewed`
label after `.snap` files land (`gh pr edit <num> --add-label "snapshot-reviewed"`).

## Resolved decisions (from plan review)

1. **Case folding:** use `ars_i18n::to_lowercase(text, locale)`. Matches the
   `ars-collections::typeahead.rs:255,297` precedent; no new public API in
   `ars-i18n`. Spec wording at `highlight.md:65` is reworded in this PR to
   describe the helper rather than the literal `CaseMapper::fold*` names.
2. **API surface:** ship the standard utility-component shape — `Part` enum,
   `Api` struct, `root_attrs` / `chunk_attrs(bool)` / `chunks(locale)`
   methods, and `ConnectApi` impl — **alongside** the standalone
   `highlight_chunks` function the spec defines. Satisfies both the spec
   template (`foundation/10` §4.1.2) and the issue's `connect()/Api`
   `AttrMap` snapshot acceptance criterion.
3. **Spec drift fixes:** both items under "Spec conformance notes" are
   addressed in this same PR (line 65 wording, line 111 missing `&locale`).

| 22 | `highlight_root_snapshot` | `root_attrs()` AttrMap. |
| 23 | `highlight_chunk_highlighted_snapshot` | `chunk_attrs(true)`. |
| 24 | `highlight_chunk_not_highlighted_snapshot` | `chunk_attrs(false)`. |
| 25 | `highlight_chunks_contains_en_snapshot` | `highlight_chunks` output for canonical Contains case. |
| 26 | `highlight_chunks_starts_with_snapshot` | Starts-with case. |
| 27 | `highlight_chunks_fuzzy_snapshot` | Fuzzy case. |
| 28 | `highlight_chunks_multi_query_merged_snapshot` | Two overlapping queries. |
| 29 | `highlight_chunks_turkic_snapshot` | Turkic case folding. |
| 30 | `highlight_chunks_german_eszett_snapshot` | German eszett. |

PR must apply the `snapshot-reviewed` label after committing the snapshots
(per `CLAUDE.md` "Development Workflow" item 10).

## Key existing utilities to reuse

| Reusable | Where | How |
|---|---|---|
| `ars_i18n::to_lowercase(text, locale)` | `crates/ars-i18n/src/case.rs:70` | Locale-aware lowercase using ICU4X `CaseMapper`; matches `typeahead.rs:255` precedent. |
| `ars_i18n::Locale` | `crates/ars-i18n/src/locale.rs` | Function parameter. |
| `ars_core::{AttrMap, ComponentPart, ConnectApi, HasId, HtmlAttr}` | `crates/ars-core/src/lib.rs` | Standard utility-component scaffolding (same imports as `separator.rs:12`). |
| Insta snapshot pattern | `crates/ars-components/src/utility/separator.rs:418–444` | `snapshot_attrs` helper + `assert_snapshot!`. |

## Spec conformance notes

The Highlight spec has two small inconsistencies worth flagging — both
should be **fixed in this PR** alongside the implementation per `CLAUDE.md`
"Spec/code mismatch resolution":

1. **`spec/components/utility/highlight.md:65`** — references
   `CaseMapper::fold_turkic()` / `CaseMapper::fold()`. We use
   `ars_i18n::to_lowercase` (which wraps `CaseMapper::lowercase_to_string`,
   handles Turkic via `language_identifier`). The acceptance criterion
   "Unicode case folding uses ICU4X CaseMapper, not ASCII-only lowering" is
   satisfied — the comment in the spec should be reworded to describe the
   helper used (or the helper renamed). Proposed edit: replace the explicit
   ICU4X method names with "delegate to the locale-aware case helpers in
   `ars-i18n`, which use ICU4X `CaseMapper` under the hood."
2. **`spec/components/utility/highlight.md:111`** — the adapter example
   calls `highlight_chunks(&props)` (one arg). Function signature is
   `(&props, &locale)`. Update the example to pass `&locale`.

Both are doc-only edits in the same component spec file. No other spec files
touch this component.

## Verification (end-to-end)

After implementation, run **in this order**:

```bash
# 1. Focused tests for the new module
cargo test -p ars-components --lib utility::highlight

# 2. Review and accept snapshots, then re-run (must remain green)
cargo insta review
cargo test -p ars-components --lib utility::highlight

# 3. Module-level lints / docs
cargo clippy -p ars-components --all-features -- -D warnings
cargo doc -p ars-components --no-deps

# 4. Coverage spot-check on the new module
cargo llvm-cov test -p ars-components --text -- utility::highlight

# 5. Spec validator picks up frontmatter / manifest consistency
cargo run -p spec-tool -- validate

# 6. Full pre-PR gate (after user approval)
cargo xci
```

PR body MUST include `Closes #204`; PR MUST get the `snapshot-reviewed`
label after `.snap` files land (`gh pr edit <num> --add-label "snapshot-reviewed"`).

## Resolved decisions (from plan review)

1. **Case folding:** use `ars_i18n::to_lowercase(text, locale)`. Matches the
   `ars-collections::typeahead.rs:255,297` precedent; no new public API in
   `ars-i18n`. Spec wording at `highlight.md:65` is reworded in this PR to
   describe the helper rather than the literal `CaseMapper::fold*` names.
2. **API surface:** ship the standard utility-component shape — `Part` enum,
   `Api` struct, `root_attrs` / `chunk_attrs(bool)` / `chunks(locale)`
   methods, and `ConnectApi` impl — **alongside** the standalone
   `highlight_chunks` function the spec defines. Satisfies both the spec
   template (`foundation/10` §4.1.2) and the issue's `connect()/Api`
   `AttrMap` snapshot acceptance criterion.
3. **Spec drift fixes:** both items under "Spec conformance notes" are
   addressed in this same PR (line 65 wording, line 111 missing `&locale`).

=== RUST SKILLS DISPLAY FORMAT ===
When showing Rust Skills loaded, display in this EXACT order:
1. FIRST: "🦀 Rust Skills Loaded" text
2. THEN: The Ferris crab ASCII art BELOW the text
The text must be ABOVE the crab, not below.
===

=== MANDATORY: META-COGNITION ROUTING ===

CRITICAL: You MUST follow the COMPLETE meta-cognition framework.
Partial compliance (only loading L1 skill) is NOT acceptable.

## STEP 1: IDENTIFY ENTRY LAYER + DOMAIN

### Layer 1 Signals (Start here, trace UP):
- Error codes: E0382, E0597, E0277, E0499, etc.
- Keywords: cannot be sent, moved value, borrowed, lifetime

### Layer 3 Domain Signals (MUST also load domain skill):

| Keywords in Question | Domain Skill to Load |
|---------------------|---------------------|
| Web API, HTTP, REST, axum, actix, handler, router | domain-web |
| payment, trading, fintech, decimal, currency | domain-fintech |
| CLI, command line, clap, terminal | domain-cli |
| embedded, no_std, MCU, firmware | domain-embedded |
| kubernetes, docker, grpc, microservice | domain-cloud-native |
| MQTT, sensor, IoT, telemetry | domain-iot |
| tensor, model, inference, ML | domain-ml |

**CRITICAL**: If domain keywords are present, you MUST load BOTH:
1. The Layer 1 skill (e.g., m07-concurrency)
2. The Layer 3 domain skill (e.g., domain-web)

## STEP 2: EXECUTE TRACING (MANDATORY)

For Layer 1 entry with domain context:

```
L1 Error (e.g., Rc not Send)
    ^ TRACE UP: Why this design constraint?
    ^ Load domain skill (e.g., domain-web)
    ^ Find domain constraint (e.g., "handlers run on any thread")
    v TRACE DOWN: What pattern satisfies constraint?
    v Return to L1 with context-aware solution
```

## STEP 3: MANDATORY OUTPUT FORMAT

Your response MUST include ALL of these sections:

### Reasoning Chain
```
+-- Layer 1: [specific error]
|   Problem: [error description]
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: [domain name] (domain-xxx)
|   Constraint: [what is the domain constraint]
|   Rule: [why this constraint exists]
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: [design decision based on domain constraint]
```

### Domain Constraints Analysis
- MUST reference specific rules from domain-xxx skill
- Explain WHY this domain has this constraint

### Recommended Solution
- Provide solution that follows domain best practices
- Not just fixing the compile error

## EXAMPLE: Web API + Send Error

Question: "Web API config sharing error: Rc cannot be sent"

CORRECT Response:
```
### Reasoning Chain
+-- Layer 1: Send/Sync Error
|   Problem: Rc<T> cannot be sent between threads
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: Web Domain (domain-web)
|   Constraint: Handlers run on any thread
|   Rule: Shared state must be thread-safe
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: Use Arc<T> + State extractor

### Domain Constraints Analysis
From domain-web:
- "Rc in state" is a Common Mistake
- Web handlers require Send + Sync
- Recommended: axum State<Arc<T>> pattern

### Recommended Solution
[Code following Web best practices]
```

WRONG Response (stops at L1):
```
Problem: Rc is not Send
Solution: Use Arc
```

## SKILLS TO INVOKE

Always invoke with Skill() tool:
- Skill(rust-router) - First, to get routing
- Skill(m0x-xxx) - Layer 1 skill based on error
- Skill(domain-xxx) - Layer 3 skill based on domain keywords

===================================

1	//! `VisuallyHidden` component machine and connect API.
2	//!
3	//! `VisuallyHidden` is a stateless attribute mapper that renders content which
4	//! is invisible on screen but fully accessible to screen readers. It has no
5	//! state machine — the framework-agnostic core consists solely of `Props`,
6	//! `Part`, and `Api`, which produces either the always-hidden CSS class or
7	//! the focus-visible data hook on the root element.
8	//!
9	//! See `spec/components/utility/visually-hidden.md` for the authoritative
10	//! contract.
11	
12	use alloc::string::String;
13	
14	use ars_core::{AttrMap, ComponentPart, ConnectApi, HasId, HtmlAttr};
15	
16	/// Props for the `VisuallyHidden` component.
17	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default, PartialEq, HasId)]
18	pub struct Props {
19	    /// Component instance ID.
20	    pub id: String,
21	
22	    /// When `true`, renders the visually-hidden styles onto the single child
23	    /// element rather than wrapping it in a `<span>`. The flag is read by
24	    /// the framework adapter to choose the render path; the agnostic-core
25	    /// attribute output is invariant under this flag.
26	    pub as_child: bool,
27	
28	    /// When `true`, the element becomes visible when it receives focus.
29	    /// Enables skip-link patterns where hidden navigation aids appear on
30	    /// focus. Default: `false`.
31	    pub is_focusable: bool,
32	}
33	
34	impl Props {
35	    /// Returns fresh [`Props`] with the documented defaults — equivalent
36	    /// to [`Default::default`], offered as the entry point for the
37	    /// builder chain.
38	    #[must_use]
39	    pub fn new() -> Self {
40	        Self::default()
41	    }
42	
43	    /// Sets the component instance ID.
44	    #[must_use]
45	    pub fn id(mut self, id: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
46	        self.id = id.into();
47	        self
48	    }
49	
50	    /// Sets whether the agnostic core renders its attributes onto a
51	    /// consumer-provided child element (`as_child` pattern) instead of
52	    /// wrapping content in a `<span>`. Adapter-only flag — the
53	    /// agnostic-core attribute output is invariant under this value.
54	    #[must_use]
55	    pub const fn as_child(mut self, value: bool) -> Self {
56	        self.as_child = value;
57	        self
58	    }
59	
60	    /// Sets whether the element becomes visible when it receives focus
61	    /// (skip-link / focus-reveal mode).
62	    #[must_use]
63	    pub const fn is_focusable(mut self, value: bool) -> Self {
64	        self.is_focusable = value;
65	        self
66	    }
67	}
68	
69	/// DOM parts of the `VisuallyHidden` component.
70	#[derive(ComponentPart)]
71	#[scope = "visually-hidden"]
72	pub enum Part {
73	    /// The root element. Adapters render `<span>` by default, or apply the
74	    /// component attributes onto the single consumer-provided child element
75	    /// when [`Props::as_child`] is `true`.
76	    Root,
77	}
78	
79	/// The API for the `VisuallyHidden` component.
80	///

[package]
name                   = "ars-collections"
version                = "0.1.0"
edition.workspace      = true
license.workspace      = true
description.workspace  = true
repository.workspace   = true
rust-version.workspace = true

[features]
default = ["std"]
std     = ["dep:ars-interactions", "indexmap/std"]
i18n    = ["dep:ars-i18n", "dep:icu"]
uuid    = ["dep:uuid"]
serde   = ["dep:serde", "indexmap/serde", "uuid?/serde"]

[dependencies]
ars-a11y         = { path = "../ars-a11y", default-features = false }
ars-core         = { path = "../ars-core", default-features = false }
ars-derive       = { path = "../ars-derive" }
ars-i18n         = { path = "../ars-i18n", optional = true }
ars-interactions = { path = "../ars-interactions", default-features = false, optional = true }
hashbrown        = { version = "0.17", default-features = false, features = ["default-hasher"] }
icu              = { version = "2.2", default-features = false, features = ["compiled_data"], optional = true }
indexmap         = { version = "2", default-features = false }
serde            = { version = "1", default-features = false, features = ["alloc", "derive"], optional = true }
uuid             = { version = "1", default-features = false, optional = true }

[dev-dependencies]
serde_json = "1"
trybuild   = "1"

[dev-dependencies.ars-interactions]
path             = "../ars-interactions"
default-features = false
features         = ["test-support"]

[lints]
workspace = true

content.subject: Write Highlight unit + snapshot tests first (TDD)
content.description: Add the full test suite (tests #1–#30 from plan) to crates/ars-components/src/utility/highlight.rs before any implementation logic. Tests must compile but should be expected to fail until the implementation lands.

content.subject: Implement Props, MatchStrategy, HighlightChunk, Part, Api, highlight_chunks
content.description: Write the highlight.rs module per plan: public types, attribute helpers, and the matching pipeline (Contains/StartsWith/Fuzzy + multi-query merge + Unicode case folding via ars_i18n::to_lowercase).

content.subject: Register module + run focused tests and snapshots

396	fn run_check(message_format: Option<&str>) -> Result<(), Error> {
397	    // Exclude ars-i18n: its `icu4x` and `web-intl` features are mutually
398	    // exclusive, so `--all-features` would trigger a compile_error!.
399	    // Instead, check ars-i18n twice — once per backend — to cover all features.
400	    cargo_with_format(
401	        Step::Check,
402	        &[
403	            "check",
404	            "--workspace",
405	            "--all-features",
406	            "--exclude",
407	            "ars-i18n",
408	        ],
409	        message_format,
410	    )?;
411	
412	    let [icu4x_features, web_intl_features] = i18n::i18n_feature_lists().map_err(Error::Io)?;
413	
414	    for features in [&icu4x_features, &web_intl_features] {
415	        cargo_with_format(
416	            Step::Check,
417	            &[
418	                "check",
419	                "-p",
420	                "ars-i18n",
421	                "--all-targets",
422	                "--no-default-features",
423	                "--features",
424	                features,
425	            ],
426	            message_format,
427	        )?;
428	    }
429	
430	    Ok(())
431	}
432	
433	fn run_clippy(message_format: Option<&str>) -> Result<(), Error> {
434	    clippy_workspace(message_format, true)
435	}
436	
437	/// Run workspace-wide clippy with ars-i18n backend splitting.
438	///
439	/// Used by both the CI `clippy` step (`deny_warnings = true`) and
440	/// `cargo xclippy` for development (`deny_warnings = false`).
441	///
442	/// # Errors
443	///
444	/// Returns [`Error::StepFailed`] if any clippy invocation exits non-zero,
445	/// or [`Error::Io`] if `crates/ars-i18n/Cargo.toml` cannot be read.
446	pub fn clippy_workspace(message_format: Option<&str>, deny_warnings: bool) -> Result<(), Error> {
447	    // Exclude ars-i18n: its `icu4x` and `web-intl` features are mutually
448	    // exclusive, so `--all-features` would trigger a compile_error!.
449	    // Instead, lint ars-i18n twice — once per backend — to cover all features.
450	    let mut workspace_args = vec![
451	        "clippy",
452	        "--workspace",
453	        "--all-targets",
454	        "--all-features",
455	        "--exclude",
456	        "ars-i18n",
457	    ];
458	
459	    if deny_warnings {
460	        workspace_args.extend_from_slice(&["--", "-D", "warnings"]);
461	    }
462	
463	    cargo_with_format(Step::Clippy, &workspace_args, message_format)?;
464	
465	    let [icu4x_features, web_intl_features] = i18n::i18n_feature_lists().map_err(Error::Io)?;
466	
467	    for features in [&icu4x_features, &web_intl_features] {
468	        let mut args = vec![
469	            "clippy",
470	            "-p",
471	            "ars-i18n",
472	            "--all-targets",
473	            "--no-default-features",
474	            "--features",
475	            features.as_str(),
476	        ];
477	
478	        if deny_warnings {
479	            args.extend_from_slice(&["--", "-D", "warnings"]);
480	        }
481	
482	        cargo_with_format(Step::Clippy, &args, message_format)?;
483	    }
484	
485	    Ok(())
486	}

509	            "ars-i18n",

The plan said no Cargo.toml changes, but that was based on an assumption I should have verified. I'll note this in the summary; it's a feature flag, not a new dependency. Now writing the highlight module.

[package]
name                   = "ars-components"
version                = "0.1.0"
edition.workspace      = true
rust-version.workspace = true
license.workspace      = true
description            = "Framework-agnostic component machines for ars-ui"
repository.workspace   = true

[features]
default = ["std"]
std = [
  "ars-collections/std",
  "ars-core/std",
  "ars-forms/std",
  "ars-i18n/std",
  "ars-interactions/std"
]
debug = ["dep:log", "ars-core/debug", "ars-interactions/debug"]
uuid = ["ars-collections/uuid"]

[dependencies]
ars-a11y         = { path = "../ars-a11y", default-features = false }
ars-collections  = { path = "../ars-collections", default-features = false }
ars-core         = { path = "../ars-core", default-features = false }
ars-forms        = { path = "../ars-forms", default-features = false }
ars-i18n         = { path = "../ars-i18n", default-features = false }
ars-interactions = { path = "../ars-interactions", default-features = false }
log              = { version = "0.4", default-features = false, optional = true }

[dev-dependencies]
insta    = { version = "1", features = ["yaml"] }
proptest = "1"

[lints]
workspace = true

61 /// Returns the text split into alternating highlighted/non-highlighted segments. 62 /// 63 /// When `ignore_case` is `true`, the implementation MUST use Unicode case folding 64 /// rather than ASCII `to_lowercase()`. If `locale` is provided, use locale-aware 65 /// case folding via ICU4X `CaseMapper::fold_turkic()` for Turkic locales. Otherwise, 66 /// use default Unicode case folding (`CaseMapper::fold()`). This ensures correct 67 /// matching for Turkish dotted-I (İ/i vs I/ı), German eszett (ß/SS), and other 68 /// locale-specific case mappings. 69 pub fn highlight_chunks<'a>(props: &'a Props, locale: &Locale) -> Vec> { 70 // Returns the text split into alternating highlighted/non-highlighted segments. 71 // When queries are empty or all empty strings, returns the full text as a single 72 // non-highlighted chunk. When multiple queries match overlapping regions, the 73 // overlapping ranges are merged into a single highlighted chunk. 74 } 75 ``` 76 77 ## 2. Anatomy 78 79 ```text 80 Highlight 81 └── Root data-ars-scope="highlight" data-ars-part="root" 82 └── Chunk | (×N, per highlight_chunks output) 83 ``` 84 85 | Part | Element | Key Attributes | 86 | ----- | ----------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | 87 | Root | `` | `data-ars-scope="highlight"`, `data-ars-part="root"` | 88 | Chunk | ``/`` | `data-ars-part="highlight-chunk"`, `data-ars-highlighted="true\|false"` | 89 90 Adapters render each `HighlightChunk` as a `` (when `highlighted == true`) or `` (when `highlighted == false`). 91 92 ## 3. Accessibility 93 94 ### 3.1 ARIA Roles, States, and Properties 95 96 - `` elements are used for highlighted text. Screen readers announce `` content as "highlighted" in most browser/AT combinations. 97 - No ARIA attributes needed — the semantic `` element conveys the highlighting. 98 - For custom styling, adapters set `data-ars-part="highlight-chunk"` and `data-ars-highlighted="true|false"` on each chunk. 99 - When using fuzzy matching (`MatchStrategy::Fuzzy`), many small `` elements may be generated. Each `` is announced as 'highlighted' by screen readers, which can create excessive verbosity. For fuzzy matches with many small highlights, adapters SHOULD consolidate adjacent highlighted chunks into a single `` element, or provide a summary announcement (e.g., 'N characters matched') instead of wrapping each character individually. 100 101 ### 3.2 Bidirectional Text 102 103 When highlighting text in mixed-direction content (e.g., English query in Arabic text), chunk boundaries may disrupt BiDi ordering. The adapter SHOULD set `dir="auto"` on the root container element, and consider applying `unicode-bidi: isolate` on individual `` and `` elements to prevent chunk boundaries from affecting text reordering. 104 105 ## 4. Adapter Rendering 106 107 ```rust 108 // Leptos example: 109 view! { 110 111 {highlight_chunks(&props).into_iter().map(|chunk| { 112 if chunk.highlighted { 113 view! { {chunk.text} } 114 } else { 115 view! { {chunk.text} } 116 } 117 }).collect_view()} 118 119 } 120 ```

lines: //! `Highlight` is a stateless utility that splits a text into alternating
//! highlighted / non-highlighted chunks based on one or more search queries.
-//! when the `i18n` feature is enabled (locale-aware lowercasing handles Turkic
-//! dotted/dotless I, German eszett, Greek final sigma, etc.), and multi-query
+//! Unicode-aware case folding via ICU4X `CaseMapper` when the `i18n` feature
+//! is enabled (locale-aware lowercasing handles Turkic dotted/dotless I,
+//! German eszett, Greek final sigma, etc.), and multi-query overlap merging.
//! The component has no state machine. Adapters render each returned
-//! attribute mapper, and the [`highlight_chunks`] standalone computation.

content.text:
## Implementation complete — ready for review

### Files

| File | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `crates/ars-components/src/utility/highlight.rs` | new (1114 lines, tests included) | `Props`, `MatchStrategy`, `HighlightChunk<'a>`, `Part`, `Api`, standalone `highlight_chunks` per spec. 36 tests. |
| `crates/ars-components/src/utility/mod.rs` | +4 lines | `pub mod highlight;` registration. |
| `crates/ars-components/Cargo.toml` | +6 lines | New `i18n = ["ars-i18n/icu4x"]` feature (not in defaults). |
| `spec/components/utility/highlight.md` | minor edit | (a) line 65 wording → describe `ars_i18n::to_lowercase`/`CaseMapper::lowercase_to_string` (the actual ICU4X 2.x API); (b) line 111 example → pass `&locale`. |
| `crates/ars-components/src/utility/snapshots/ars_components__utility__highlight__tests__*.snap` | 9 new | All accepted via `cargo insta accept`. |

### Verification

- `cargo test -p ars-components --lib --features i18n utility::highlight`: **36/36 pass** (incl. Turkic + German eszett).
- `cargo test -p ars-components --lib utility::highlight` (no `i18n`): **32/32 pass** (4 ICU4X-gated tests cfg-skipped).
- `cargo test -p ars-components --features i18n --all-targets`: **1499 lib tests pass** (was 1463 — +36 from highlight; no regressions in other modules).
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features --exclude ars-i18n -- -D warnings`: clean.
- `cargo doc -p ars-components --no-deps --all-features`: zero highlight-related warnings.
- `cargo llvm-cov`: `utility/highlight.rs` at **98.67% line / 100% function / 99.16% region** (workspace threshold for `ars-components` is 90/90).
- `cargo xtask spec validate`: 340 files validated, all checks passed.
- `cargo insta test … --unreferenced=reject`: no orphan snapshots.

### One deviation from the approved plan

The plan said "No Cargo.toml changes". I had to add an `i18n` feature flag because `ars_i18n::to_lowercase` is gated behind `ars-i18n/icu4x`, and `ars-components` declares `ars-i18n = { default-features = false }`, so it wasn't reachable without a flag. I added `i18n = ["ars-i18n/icu4x"]` as a **non-default** feature so:

- `cargo test --all-features` (CI default) exercises the ICU4X path.
- Adapters that want the `web-intl` backend (a `--no-default-features` build) aren't forced into mutual-exclusion conflict with `icu4x`.
- Without `i18n` the module still compiles — it transparently falls back to `str::to_lowercase`, with the cost being Turkic / Greek / Lithuanian language-tailored mappings no longer apply. Those four spec-mandated tests are `#[cfg(feature = "i18n")]`-gated.

This isn't a new crate dependency (no `Cargo.toml [dependencies]` additions); it just exposes the existing `ars-i18n/icu4x` feature through a new `ars-components` feature.

Nothing committed or pushed yet. Per CLAUDE.md workflow item 7, awaiting your approval before `cargo xci` + commit + PR. Once you approve I'll run `cargo xci`, commit, push, open the PR with `Closes #204`, and apply the `snapshot-reviewed` label.

In those recommendations, I want you to totally disregard the spec and the current implementation, the recommendation should be the best outcome as possible. Sometimes the best outcome may the spec, other times it will be the current implementation, some other times it will be a mix of both, and eventually it can also be a totally different thing

attachment.content:
=== RUST SKILLS DISPLAY FORMAT ===
When showing Rust Skills loaded, display in this EXACT order:
1. FIRST: "🦀 Rust Skills Loaded" text
2. THEN: The Ferris crab ASCII art BELOW the text
The text must be ABOVE the crab, not below.
===

=== MANDATORY: META-COGNITION ROUTING ===

CRITICAL: You MUST follow the COMPLETE meta-cognition framework.
Partial compliance (only loading L1 skill) is NOT acceptable.

## STEP 1: IDENTIFY ENTRY LAYER + DOMAIN

### Layer 1 Signals (Start here, trace UP):
- Error codes: E0382, E0597, E0277, E0499, etc.
- Keywords: cannot be sent, moved value, borrowed, lifetime

### Layer 3 Domain Signals (MUST also load domain skill):

| Keywords in Question | Domain Skill to Load |
|---------------------|---------------------|
| Web API, HTTP, REST, axum, actix, handler, router | domain-web |
| payment, trading, fintech, decimal, currency | domain-fintech |
| CLI, command line, clap, terminal | domain-cli |
| embedded, no_std, MCU, firmware | domain-embedded |
| kubernetes, docker, grpc, microservice | domain-cloud-native |
| MQTT, sensor, IoT, telemetry | domain-iot |
| tensor, model, inference, ML | domain-ml |

**CRITICAL**: If domain keywords are present, you MUST load BOTH:
1. The Layer 1 skill (e.g., m07-concurrency)
2. The Layer 3 domain skill (e.g., domain-web)

## STEP 2: EXECUTE TRACING (MANDATORY)

For Layer 1 entry with domain context:

```
L1 Error (e.g., Rc not Send)
    ^ TRACE UP: Why this design constraint?
    ^ Load domain skill (e.g., domain-web)
    ^ Find domain constraint (e.g., "handlers run on any thread")
    v TRACE DOWN: What pattern satisfies constraint?
    v Return to L1 with context-aware solution
```

## STEP 3: MANDATORY OUTPUT FORMAT

Your response MUST include ALL of these sections:

### Reasoning Chain
```
+-- Layer 1: [specific error]
|   Problem: [error description]
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: [domain name] (domain-xxx)
|   Constraint: [what is the domain constraint]
|   Rule: [why this constraint exists]
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: [design decision based on domain constraint]
```

### Domain Constraints Analysis
- MUST reference specific rules from domain-xxx skill
- Explain WHY this domain has this constraint

### Recommended Solution
- Provide solution that follows domain best practices
- Not just fixing the compile error

## EXAMPLE: Web API + Send Error

Question: "Web API config sharing error: Rc cannot be sent"

CORRECT Response:
```
### Reasoning Chain
+-- Layer 1: Send/Sync Error
|   Problem: Rc<T> cannot be sent between threads
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: Web Domain (domain-web)
|   Constraint: Handlers run on any thread
|   Rule: Shared state must be thread-safe
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: Use Arc<T> + State extractor

### Domain Constraints Analysis
From domain-web:
- "Rc in state" is a Common Mistake
- Web handlers require Send + Sync
- Recommended: axum State<Arc<T>> pattern

### Recommended Solution
[Code following Web best practices]
```

WRONG Response (stops at L1):
```
Problem: Rc is not Send
Solution: Use Arc
```

## SKILLS TO INVOKE

Always invoke with Skill() tool:
- Skill(rust-router) - First, to get routing
- Skill(m0x-xxx) - Layer 1 skill based on error
- Skill(domain-xxx) - Layer 3 skill based on domain keywords

===================================

It has no state machine — the adapter renders `` elements for highlighted chunks. 15 16 ## 1. API 17 18 ### 1.1 Props 19 20 ```rust 21 /// Props for the `Highlight` component. 22 #[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)] 23 pub struct Props { 24 /// The search queries to highlight. Supports a single query string or 25 /// multiple queries to highlight simultaneously (e.g., multiple search 26 /// terms). When multiple queries are provided, all matches from all 27 /// queries are highlighted; overlapping matches are merged. 28 pub query: Vec, 29 /// The full text to search within. 30 pub text: String, 31 /// Case-insensitive matching (default: true). 32 pub ignore_case: bool, 33 /// Matching strategy. 34 pub match_strategy: MatchStrategy, 35 } 36 37 /// The strategy to use for matching the query within the text. 38 #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq)] 39 pub enum MatchStrategy { 40 /// Highlight all occurrences of the query within the text. 41 Contains, 42 /// Highlight only if the text starts with the query. 43 StartsWith, 44 /// Highlight individual characters for fuzzy matching. 45 Fuzzy, 46 } 47 ``` 48 49 ### 1.2 Connect / API 50 51 `Highlight` uses a function-based API that returns text chunks rather than the standard `ConnectApi` / `AttrMap` pattern. The adapter renders each chunk as a DOM element. 52 53 ```rust 54 /// A chunk of text that has been highlighted or not. 55 #[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)] 56 pub struct HighlightChunk<'a> { 57 pub text: &'a str, 58 pub highlighted: bool, 59 } 60 61 /// Returns the text split into alternating highlighted/non-highlighted segments. 62 /// 63 /// When `ignore_case` is `true`, the implementation MUST use locale-aware Unicode 64 /// case mapping rather than ASCII `to_lowercase()`. The agnostic-core implementation 65 /// delegates to `ars_i18n::to_lowercase`, which wraps ICU4X 66 /// `CaseMapper::lowercase_to_string` with the locale's language identifier. This 67 /// ensures correct matching for Turkish dotted/dotless I (`İ`/`i` vs `I`/`ı`), 68 /// German eszett (`ß`/`SS`), Greek final sigma, Lithuanian dotted I, and other 69 /// locale-tailored case mappings supplied by CLDR. 70 pub fn highlight_chunks<'a>(props: &'a Props, locale: &Locale) -> Vec> { 71 // Returns the text split into alternating highlighted/non-highlighted segments. 72 // When queries are empty or all empty strings, returns the full text as a single 73 // non-highlighted chunk. When multiple queries match overlapping regions, the 74 // overlapping ranges are merged into a single highlighted chunk. 75 } 76 ``` 77 78 ## 2. Anatomy 79 80 ```text 81 Highlight 82 └── Root data-ars-scope="highlight" data-ars-part="root" 83 └── Chunk | (×N, per highlight_chunks output) 84 ``` 85 86 | Part | Element | Key Attributes | 87 | ----- | ----------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | 88 | Root | `` | `data-ars-scope="highlight"`, `data-ars-part="root"` | 89 | Chunk | ``/`` | `data-ars-part="highlight-chunk"`, `data-ars-highlighted="true\|false"` | 90 91 Adapters render each `HighlightChunk` as a `` (when `highlighted == true`) or `` (when `highlighted == false`). 92 93 ## 3. Accessibility 94 95 ### 3.1 ARIA Roles, States, and Properties 96 97 - `` elements are used for highlighted text. Screen readers announce `` content as "highlighted" in most browser/AT combinations. 98 - No ARIA attributes needed — the semantic `` element conveys the highlighting. 99 - For custom styling, adapters set `data-ars-part="highlight-chunk"` and `data-ars-highlighted="true|false"` on each chunk. 100 - When using fuzzy matching (`MatchStrategy::Fuzzy`), many small `` elements may be generated. Each `` is announced as 'highlighted' by screen readers, which can create excessive verbosity. For fuzzy matches with many small highlights, adapters SHOULD consolidate adjacent highlighted chunks into a single `` element, or provide a summary announcement (e.g., 'N characters matched') instead of wrapping each character individually. 101 102 ### 3.2 Bidirectional Text 103 104 When highlighting text in mixed-direction content (e.g., English query in Arabic text), chunk boundaries may disrupt BiDi ordering. The adapter SHOULD set `dir="auto"` on the root container element, and consider applying `unicode-bidi: isolate` on individual `` and `` elements to prevent chunk boundaries from affecting text reordering. 105 106 ## 4. Adapter Rendering 107 108 ```rust 109 // Leptos example: 110 view! { 111 112 {highlight_chunks(&props, &locale).into_iter().map(|chunk| { 113 if chunk.highlighted { 114 view! { {chunk.text} } 115 } else { 116 view! { {chunk.text} } 117 } 118 }).collect_view()} 119 120 } 121 ``` 122 123 ## 5. Library Parity 124 125 > Compared against: Ark UI (`Highlight`). 126 127 ### 5.1 Props 128 129 | Feature | ars-ui | Ark UI | Notes | 130 | -------------- | ------------------------------- | --------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | 131 | Query | `query: Vec` | `query: string \| string[]` | Both support multiple queries | 132 | Text | `text` | `text` | Both libraries | 133 | Ignore case | `ignore_case` | `ignoreCase` | Both libraries | 134 | Match strategy | `match_strategy: MatchStrategy` | `exactMatch`, `matchAll` | ars-ui uses enum; Ark uses boolean flags | 135 | Locale | `locale` | -- | ars-ui addition for locale-aware case folding | 136 137 **Gaps:** None. 138 139 ### 5.2 Anatomy 140 141 | Part | ars-ui | Ark UI | Notes | 142 | ----- | ------------------- | ------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | 143 | Root | `Root` | (inline rendering) | Both produce chunks for rendering | 144 | Chunk | `` / `` | `` / `` | Both libraries use mark for highlighted text | 145 146 **Gaps:** None. 147 148 ### 5.3 Features 149 150 | Feature | ars-ui | Ark UI | 151 | -------------------- | --------------------------------- | ------ | 152 | Multi-query | Yes | Yes | 153 | Case-insensitive | Yes | Yes | 154 | Fuzzy matching | Yes (`MatchStrategy::Fuzzy`) | -- | 155 | StartsWith matching | Yes (`MatchStrategy::StartsWith`) | -- | 156 | Locale-aware folding | Yes | -- | 157 158 **Gaps:** None. 159 160 ### 5.4 Summary 161 162 - **Overall:** Full parity -- ars-ui is a superset. 163 - **Divergences:** ars-ui uses `MatchStrategy` enum (Contains/StartsWith/Fuzzy) while Ark uses `exactMatch`/`matchAll` booleans. ars-ui adds locale-aware Unicode case folding and fuzzy matching. 164 - **Recommended additions:** None. 165

# Spec Directory Guide

## File Conventions

- `_category.md` files contain category overviews and shared conventions
- Component files use kebab-case matching the component name
- Shared types in `shared/` are referenced by component frontmatter
- Headings are numbered (`## 1. State Machine`, `### 1.1 States`)

## Spec Changes During Implementation

The repo is in active implementation. Spec edits inside `spec/` should usually be tied to a concrete GitHub implementation task or PR, not made as detached cleanup.

- Read the implementation issue before changing spec text.
- Keep cited spec sections synchronized with the implementation task that discovered the mismatch.
- If an adapter-specific implementation exposes a missing shared abstraction, promote that abstraction into the appropriate foundation/shared spec instead of documenting it only in framework code.
- Do not leave placeholder notes for later reconciliation; resolve the spec/code mismatch in the same task whenever possible.

## Adding a New Component

1. Create `components/{category}/{component-name}.md` with YAML frontmatter
2. Follow the section structure defined in `foundation/10-component-spec-template.md` for the appropriate tier
3. Add an entry to `manifest.toml` with path, category, deps, and related
4. Update `foundation/02-component-catalog.md` with the new component
5. Run `cargo run -p spec-tool -- validate` to verify consistency

## Component File Format

```markdown
---
component: ComponentName
category: { category }
tier: stateless | stateful | complex
foundation_deps: [architecture, accessibility, ...]
shared_deps: [date-time-types, ...]
related: [sibling-component, ...]
---

# ComponentName
```

See `foundation/10-component-spec-template.md` for the canonical section structure, ordering rules, conformance checklist, and skeleton examples for each tier.

lastPrompt: Great, now let's do an audit on how well our new code here follows our spec, do an in depth check for spec drift and suggest recomendations on how to proceed for each finding. In those recommendations…

content.text:
# Spec ↔ Implementation Drift Audit

Read both files end-to-end and traced every public surface. Findings below are grouped by severity. The "Best outcome" column ignores which side is currently right and picks the design I'd want if I were starting clean.

---

## High-severity drift

### H1 — `Api` / `ConnectApi` exists despite spec opting out

| | |
|---|---|
| Spec says | §1.2: *"Highlight uses a function-based API that returns text chunks **rather than** the standard `ConnectApi` / `AttrMap` pattern."* |
| Impl does | Provides `Part::Root`, `pub struct Api { props }`, `Api::root_attrs`, `Api::chunk_attrs(bool)`, `Api::chunks(locale)`, `impl ConnectApi for Api`, plus accessor methods. |
| Why it drifted | Issue acceptance criteria said *"Snapshot tests cover the component's `connect()` / `Api` `AttrMap` output for every anatomy part"* — pure-function form has no AttrMap to snapshot. The stateless-tier template (`foundation/10` §4.1.2) also requires `Part` + `Api`. |
| **Best outcome** | **Keep impl, fix spec.** A small `Api` is genuinely useful here: it gives adapters one canonical source for `data-ars-scope` / `data-ars-part` / `data-ars-highlighted` strings instead of inlining them. Spec §1.2's "rather than" language is wrong — the right framing is *"function-based output for chunks, plus a thin attribute mapper for the root and per-chunk data attributes."* Rewrite §1.2 to show both. |

### H2 — Anatomy table lists `Chunk` as a part, `Part` enum doesn't

| | |
|---|---|
| Spec says | §2 anatomy table has two rows: `Root` and `Chunk`. |
| Impl does | `Part::Root` only. `Part::Chunk` is intentionally absent because chunk attrs depend on a runtime bool, and `ConnectApi::part_attrs(Part)` takes the variant by value with no payload. The dynamic slot is served by `Api::chunk_attrs(bool)`. |
| **Best outcome** | **Keep impl, refine spec.** Anatomy parts come in two shapes: *static slots* (one DOM element per component instance, expressible as a `Part` variant) and *parametric slots* (zero-to-N elements per instance, parameterised on runtime data). `Chunk` is the latter. Spec §2 should keep the row but annotate it: *"Chunk is a parametric anatomy slot — its attributes are produced by `Api::chunk_attrs(highlighted: bool)` rather than via the static `Part` enum."* This is a pattern that will recur (table rows, virtualised lists, tags-input chips) so naming it in `foundation/10` would pay off across the catalog. |

---

## Medium-severity drift

### M1 — Adjacent ranges are merged in the core, spec puts that on adapters

| | |
|---|---|
| Spec says | §3.1: *"For fuzzy matches with many small highlights, **adapters SHOULD consolidate** adjacent highlighted chunks into a single `<mark>` element..."* |
| Impl does | `merge_ranges` already merges `start <= last.1`, which covers overlap **and** adjacency. Adapters never see back-to-back highlighted chunks in the first place. |
| **Best outcome** | **Keep impl, move the contract into the core in the spec.** Doing the consolidation in the core is strictly better: (a) screen readers don't hear a stutter of "highlighted, highlighted, highlighted" for adjacent fuzzy hits, (b) DOM output is leaner, (c) every adapter gets the right behaviour for free. Spec §3.1 should change from *"adapters SHOULD"* to *"the agnostic core consolidates adjacent and overlapping highlighted ranges before returning; adapters MAY layer additional summary announcements on top for high-cardinality fuzzy results."* |

### M2 — `i18n` feature flag is invisible to the spec

| | |
|---|---|
| Spec says | §1.2: *"The implementation MUST use locale-aware Unicode case mapping rather than ASCII `to_lowercase()`."* No mention of build features. |
| Impl does | Gated on `cfg(feature = "i18n")`; falls back to `str::to_lowercase` (Unicode-aware, locale-independent) when the feature is off. The Turkic / German / Greek guarantees only hold with `i18n` on. |
| Why it drifted | `ars-components` declares `ars-i18n = { default-features = false }`, so `ars_i18n::to_lowercase` isn't reachable without an explicit feature. Adapters need to support both `icu4x` and `web-intl` backends; the spec contract for Turkic was written as if locale-aware was free. |
| **Best outcome** | **Make `i18n` always on for `ars-components`, drop the fallback.** Reasons: (a) the spec contract talks in absolutes ("MUST"), so the fallback is a quiet contract violation; (b) every adapter on the roadmap pulls in ICU4X via its own default features anyway — nobody is shipping `ars-components` without it; (c) keeping the fallback doubles the code path surface (untested branch in the no-feature build is the silent failure waiting to happen). Mechanism: change `ars-components/Cargo.toml` to `ars-i18n = { ..., features = ["icu4x"] }` *only* when web-intl isn't selected, OR make the highlight module itself gated on `i18n` so the contract is "if you use Highlight, you've enabled `i18n`." The second is cleaner; the first conflicts with web-intl mutual exclusion. |

### M3 — `Default` impl, `Eq` derive, and `MatchStrategy::Contains` default are spec-invisible

| | |
|---|---|
| Spec says | `#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]` on `Props`; `#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq)]` on `MatchStrategy`. Props default values described in prose only (`"default: true"` for `ignore_case`). |
| Impl does | Adds `impl Default for Props`, `#[derive(Default)]` + `#[default] Contains` on `MatchStrategy`, `Eq` on `MatchStrategy` and `HighlightChunk`. |
| **Best outcome** | **Keep impl, lift defaults into the spec.** `Default` is the right way to encode the *"default: true"* prose — it makes the default machine-checkable. `Contains` is the right default (most permissive, matches Ark UI). `Eq` on `MatchStrategy` and `HighlightChunk` is free (no float fields) and helps test code use `assert_eq!` without thinking. The spec should derive `Default` + `Eq` on the same items and replace the prose default with a `Default` impl. |

### M4 — Builder pattern is impl-only

| | |
|---|---|
| Spec says | Nothing. |
| Impl does | `Props::new().query(["foo"]).text("...").ignore_case(false).match_strategy(MatchStrategy::Fuzzy)`. |
| **Best outcome** | **Keep impl, show the builder in spec §1.1.** Builders are the workspace convention for stateless props (separator, visually-hidden, focus_ring all have them); the spec template even allows them in §4.1.2. Surfacing the builder in the Highlight spec keeps the catalog consistent and saves adapter authors a roundtrip to read the impl. |

### M5 — Spec adapter example hardcodes data attribute strings

| | |
|---|---|
| Spec says | §4: `<span data-ars-scope="highlight" data-ars-part="root">` (literal strings). |
| Impl does | Provides `Api::root_attrs()` and `Api::chunk_attrs(bool)` so adapters spread typed attrs. |
| **Best outcome** | **Rewrite spec §4 to use the spread pattern.** If `Api::root_attrs()` exists (per H1), the spec must promote it as the canonical adapter path — otherwise adapters will copy the spec's hardcoded strings and drift when scope or part names change. Replace the literal-string example with `<span ..api.root_attrs()> ... <mark ..api.chunk_attrs(true)>{chunk.text}</mark> ...`. |

---

## Low-severity drift

### L1 — Empty-text behaviour is undefined in spec

| | |
|---|---|
| Spec says | Function comment specifies empty-query behaviour (one non-highlighted chunk) but is silent on empty-text. |
| Impl does | Empty text → empty `Vec`. |
| **Best outcome** | **Keep impl, document explicitly in spec.** `Vec::new()` is the honest answer — there's nothing to render. Returning `vec![HighlightChunk { text: "", highlighted: false }]` would force adapters to filter empty chunks downstream. Spec §1.2 doc-comment should add the empty-text case alongside the empty-query case. |

### L2 — Locale-when-ignore_case-false is undefined in spec

| | |
|---|---|
| Spec says | Nothing. |
| Impl does | Ignores the locale (and documents that). |
| **Best outcome** | **Keep impl, add one sentence to spec.** Obvious behaviour, but make it visible: *"When `ignore_case` is `false`, the `locale` parameter is ignored — matching is byte-exact."* |

### L3 — Fuzzy "all-or-nothing" semantics are undocumented

| | |
|---|---|
| Spec says | §1.1 enum doc: *"Highlight individual characters for fuzzy matching."* |
| Impl does | Requires the *entire* query subsequence to be found; if any char is missing, that query contributes zero ranges. |
| **Best outcome** | **Keep impl, document.** All-or-nothing is the right choice — partial matches would highlight stray characters that don't represent the query, which is a confusing UX. Spec doc on `MatchStrategy::Fuzzy` should say: *"All query characters must appear in order within the text, allowing arbitrary gaps. When the full subsequence is found, each matched character becomes its own one-char highlighted range. When any query character cannot be located, no fuzzy match is emitted for that query."* |

### L4 — Multi-query "overlap" vs "overlap+adjacent" is ambiguous

| | |
|---|---|
| Spec says | *"overlapping ranges are merged"* (§1.1, §1.2). |
| Impl does | Merges overlap **and** adjacency. |
| **Best outcome** | **Keep impl, sharpen spec wording.** Same direction as M1. Spec should say *"overlapping or adjacent ranges"* throughout. |

### L5 — Spec doesn't note absence of `HasId`

| | |
|---|---|
| Spec says | No `id` field, no comment about it. |
| Impl does | Notes in a doc comment that `Props` doesn't derive `HasId` because the rendered output is a sequence of chunks, not a single addressable element. |
| **Best outcome** | **Keep impl, mention in spec §1.1.** Worth one sentence so adapter authors don't go looking for an `id` field. |

### L6 — BiDi guidance lives only in spec §3.2

| | |
|---|---|
| Spec says | Adapter SHOULD set `dir="auto"` on root and consider `unicode-bidi: isolate` on chunks. |
| Impl does | Nothing — leaves it to adapter. |
| **Best outcome** | **Move part of it into the core.** `dir="auto"` is a static attribute on the root with no downside — `Api::root_attrs()` should emit `dir="auto"` so every adapter gets BiDi-safe behaviour for free, and the spec §3.2 wording would shift from *"adapter SHOULD set `dir="auto"`"* to *"the core sets `dir="auto"` on the root. Adapters MAY add `unicode-bidi: isolate` styling on chunk elements as a layered enhancement."* The `unicode-bidi: isolate` part is a CSS concern that the core can't reach, so leave that on adapters. |

---

## Spec mistakes that aren't drift but should be fixed in the same pass

### X1 — `MatchStrategy` derive list misses `Eq`

`#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq)]` is what the spec shows. The type is a simple C-like enum with no payload — `Eq` is free and would let downstream code use `==` in `const` contexts and `match` with exhaustive patterns more freely. Same for `HighlightChunk`.

### X2 — `HighlightChunk` derives in spec miss `Eq`

`#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]` — `text: &'a str` and `highlighted: bool` are both `Eq`, so add it.

### X3 — Spec §3.1's screen-reader claim is unsourced

> *"Screen readers announce `<mark>` content as 'highlighted' in most browser/AT combinations."*

This is true for VoiceOver/JAWS in some configurations and false in others (NVDA in default settings doesn't announce `<mark>` at all without ARIA help). The spec's downstream guidance ("no ARIA attributes needed") rests on this claim. Either cite the source (WAI-ARIA-Practices, ARIA in HTML, or a measured AT survey) or soften to *"`<mark>` is the semantic element for highlighted text; AT announcement varies by browser/AT combination."*

---

## Summary of recommended direction

| Where to put truth | Items |
|---|---|
| **Spec changes** (8) | H1, H2, M1, M3, M4, M5, L1, L2, L3, L4, L5, X1, X2, X3 — mostly *spec catches up to impl* + explicit defaults + adapter example uses helpers. |
| **Impl changes** (2) | L6 — add `dir="auto"` to `root_attrs()`; M2 — pick a model for `i18n` and either remove the fallback or properly document it (favour: make Highlight module gated on `i18n` and drop the fallback). |
| **Both** (1) | M2 again — paired Cargo.toml + spec changes describing the build model. |
| **Status quo is correct, just needs documentation** (4) | M3 (defaults), L1, L2, L3 (edge cases), L5 (no HasId). |

Nothing here is a correctness bug; the code does what its tests and acceptance criteria say. The drift is concentrated in two pockets:

1. **Spec wrote "rather than ConnectApi/AttrMap" while the template required ConnectApi/AttrMap.** Impl picked the template + acceptance-criteria side. The right reconciliation is *"the core has a thin attribute mapper for the root and a parameterised helper for chunks, alongside the chunk-computation function"* — and the spec gets a one-paragraph rewrite to say that.
2. **Build-feature gating is invisible to the spec.** The MUST language for ICU4X is currently violated in the `i18n`-off build. Either tighten the impl (Highlight requires `i18n`) or loosen the spec (document the fallback and what it doesn't guarantee). I'd go with tighten.

Want me to land any of these as follow-up edits to the highlight spec/impl before we open the PR, or hold for separate tasks?

content.text: Let's go with the best outcome for all those findings now, don't defer anything for later

attachment.content:
=== RUST SKILLS DISPLAY FORMAT ===
When showing Rust Skills loaded, display in this EXACT order:
1. FIRST: "🦀 Rust Skills Loaded" text
2. THEN: The Ferris crab ASCII art BELOW the text
The text must be ABOVE the crab, not below.
===

=== MANDATORY: META-COGNITION ROUTING ===

CRITICAL: You MUST follow the COMPLETE meta-cognition framework.
Partial compliance (only loading L1 skill) is NOT acceptable.

## STEP 1: IDENTIFY ENTRY LAYER + DOMAIN

### Layer 1 Signals (Start here, trace UP):
- Error codes: E0382, E0597, E0277, E0499, etc.
- Keywords: cannot be sent, moved value, borrowed, lifetime

### Layer 3 Domain Signals (MUST also load domain skill):

| Keywords in Question | Domain Skill to Load |
|---------------------|---------------------|
| Web API, HTTP, REST, axum, actix, handler, router | domain-web |
| payment, trading, fintech, decimal, currency | domain-fintech |
| CLI, command line, clap, terminal | domain-cli |
| embedded, no_std, MCU, firmware | domain-embedded |
| kubernetes, docker, grpc, microservice | domain-cloud-native |
| MQTT, sensor, IoT, telemetry | domain-iot |
| tensor, model, inference, ML | domain-ml |

**CRITICAL**: If domain keywords are present, you MUST load BOTH:
1. The Layer 1 skill (e.g., m07-concurrency)
2. The Layer 3 domain skill (e.g., domain-web)

## STEP 2: EXECUTE TRACING (MANDATORY)

For Layer 1 entry with domain context:

```
L1 Error (e.g., Rc not Send)
    ^ TRACE UP: Why this design constraint?
    ^ Load domain skill (e.g., domain-web)
    ^ Find domain constraint (e.g., "handlers run on any thread")
    v TRACE DOWN: What pattern satisfies constraint?
    v Return to L1 with context-aware solution
```

## STEP 3: MANDATORY OUTPUT FORMAT

Your response MUST include ALL of these sections:

### Reasoning Chain
```
+-- Layer 1: [specific error]
|   Problem: [error description]
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: [domain name] (domain-xxx)
|   Constraint: [what is the domain constraint]
|   Rule: [why this constraint exists]
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: [design decision based on domain constraint]
```

### Domain Constraints Analysis
- MUST reference specific rules from domain-xxx skill
- Explain WHY this domain has this constraint

### Recommended Solution
- Provide solution that follows domain best practices
- Not just fixing the compile error

## EXAMPLE: Web API + Send Error

Question: "Web API config sharing error: Rc cannot be sent"

CORRECT Response:
```
### Reasoning Chain
+-- Layer 1: Send/Sync Error
|   Problem: Rc<T> cannot be sent between threads
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: Web Domain (domain-web)
|   Constraint: Handlers run on any thread
|   Rule: Shared state must be thread-safe
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: Use Arc<T> + State extractor

### Domain Constraints Analysis
From domain-web:
- "Rc in state" is a Common Mistake
- Web handlers require Send + Sync
- Recommended: axum State<Arc<T>> pattern

### Recommended Solution
[Code following Web best practices]
```

WRONG Response (stops at L1):
```
Problem: Rc is not Send
Solution: Use Arc
```

## SKILLS TO INVOKE

Always invoke with Skill() tool:
- Skill(rust-router) - First, to get routing
- Skill(m0x-xxx) - Layer 1 skill based on error
- Skill(domain-xxx) - Layer 3 skill based on domain keywords

===================================

[package]
name                   = "ars-components"
version                = "0.1.0"
edition.workspace      = true
rust-version.workspace = true
license.workspace      = true
description            = "Framework-agnostic component machines for ars-ui"
repository.workspace   = true

[features]
default = ["std"]
std = [
  "ars-collections/std",
  "ars-core/std",
  "ars-forms/std",
  "ars-i18n/std",
  "ars-interactions/std"
]
debug = ["dep:log", "ars-core/debug", "ars-interactions/debug"]
# Enables ICU4X-backed Unicode case folding for components that need
# locale-aware matching (currently the `utility::highlight` module). When
# disabled, those components fall back to `str::to_lowercase`, which is
# Unicode-aware but locale-independent (Turkic dotted/dotless I and similar
# language-tailored mappings will not match).
i18n = ["ars-i18n/icu4x"]
uuid = ["ars-collections/uuid"]

[dependencies]
ars-a11y         = { path = "../ars-a11y", default-features = false }
ars-collections  = { path = "../ars-collections", default-features = false }

log              = { version = "0.4", default-features = false, optional = true }

[dev-dependencies]
insta    = { version = "1", features = ["yaml"] }
proptest = "1"

[lints]
workspace = true

lines: -`Highlight` is a pure computation utility that splits text into highlighted and non-highlighted chunks based on a search query. It has no state machine — the adapter renders `<mark>` elements for highlighted chunks.

+`Highlight` is a stateless utility that splits a text into alternating highlighted / non-highlighted chunks based on one or more search queries. It has no state machine. Adapters render the returned chunks as `<mark>` (highlighted) or `<span>` (non-highlighted) inside a single `<span>` root.

+`Highlight` follows the standard stateless utility shape: `Props` + `Part` + `Api`, plus a public free function (`highlight_chunks`) that does the chunk computation. Adapters spread `Api::root_attrs()` / `Api::chunk_attrs(highlighted)` onto the rendered elements and iterate `Api::chunks(locale)` (or call `highlight_chunks` directly) for the chunk sequence.
/// The search queries to highlight. Supports a single query string or
/// multiple queries to highlight simultaneously (e.g., multiple search
/// terms). When multiple queries are provided, all matches from all
-    /// queries are highlighted; overlapping matches are merged.
+    /// queries are highlighted; overlapping or adjacent ranges are merged
+    /// into a single highlighted chunk. An empty vec, or a vec containing
+    /// only empty strings, produces a single non-highlighted chunk
+    /// wrapping the full text. Default: empty.
-    /// The full text to search within.
+    /// The full text to search within. Default: empty.
-/// The strategy to use for matching the query within the text.
+/// The strategy used to match the query against the text.
+#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
-    /// Highlight all occurrences of the query within the text.
+    /// Highlight every occurrence of the query within the text. Default.
-    /// Highlight only if the text starts with the query.
+    /// Highlight only the leading run of the text when it starts with the
-    /// Highlight individual characters for fuzzy matching.
+    /// Subsequence match: highlight each individual query character within
+    /// the text in order, allowing arbitrary gaps. All query characters
+    /// must be present; if any character is missing, no fuzzy ranges are
+`Highlight` does **not** derive `HasId` and has no `id` field — the rendered output is a sequence of chunks rather than a single addressable DOM element.
-`Highlight` uses a function-based API that returns text chunks rather than the standard `ConnectApi` / `AttrMap` pattern. The adapter renders each chunk as a DOM element.
+A fluent builder is provided per the workspace stateless-utility convention (see `foundation/10-component-spec-template.md` §4.1.2):
-/// A chunk of text that has been highlighted or not.
+/// One contiguous run of the original text, tagged as highlighted or not.
+    /// The slice of the source text covered by this chunk.
+    /// Whether the adapter should wrap this chunk in `<mark>`.
-/// Returns the text split into alternating highlighted/non-highlighted segments.
-/// When `ignore_case` is `true`, the implementation MUST use locale-aware Unicode
-/// case mapping rather than ASCII `to_lowercase()`. The agnostic-core implementation
-/// `CaseMapper::lowercase_to_string` with the locale's language identifier. This
-/// ensures correct matching for Turkish dotted/dotless I (`İ`/`i` vs `I`/`ı`),
-/// German eszett (`ß`/`SS`), Greek final sigma, Lithuanian dotted I, and other
-/// locale-tailored case mappings supplied by CLDR.
-    // Returns the text split into alternating highlighted/non-highlighted segments.
-    // When queries are empty or all empty strings, returns the full text as a single
-    // non-highlighted chunk. When multiple queries match overlapping regions, the
-    // overlapping ranges are merged into a single highlighted chunk.
+/// Only `Root` is a static anatomy slot. The per-chunk `<mark>` / `<span>`
+/// elements are emitted dynamically per `HighlightChunk`, parametric on the
+/// takes a `Part` by value, no payload) cannot express. Adapters call
+    pub const fn new(props: Props) -> Self;
+    /// correct BiDi paragraph direction from the first strong character —
+    /// Attributes for one chunk element. Emits
+    /// vs `<span>`) is chosen by the adapter from the same boolean.
+    pub fn chunk_attrs(&self, highlighted: bool) -> AttrMap;
+    pub fn chunks<'a>(&'a self, locale: &Locale) -> Vec<HighlightChunk<'a>>;
+    fn part_attrs(&self, part: Part) -> AttrMap;  // Part::Root → root_attrs
+/// Splits the text into alternating highlighted / non-highlighted segments.
+/// When `ignore_case` is `true`, matching uses locale-aware Unicode case
+/// This ensures correct matching for Turkish dotted/dotless I (`İ`/`i` vs
+/// `I`/`ı`), German eszett (`ß`/`SS`), Greek final sigma, Lithuanian
+/// dotted I, and other locale-tailored case mappings supplied by CLDR.
+/// When `ignore_case` is `false`, matching is byte-exact and the `locale`
+/// When multiple queries (or fuzzy hits) produce overlapping or adjacent
+/// ranges, the agnostic core merges them into a single highlighted chunk
+/// before returning — adapters never see back-to-back `<mark>` elements
+/// - Empty `query`, or `query` containing only empty strings → one
+///   non-highlighted chunk wrapping the full text.
+/// - Query longer than text → one non-highlighted chunk.
+/// - `MatchStrategy::Fuzzy` with any missing query character → that query

+The agnostic-core lives in `ars-components` gated on `feature = "i18n"`, which enables `ars-i18n/icu4x`. The locale-aware case-mapping guarantee on `ignore_case = true` is non-negotiable, so the module is unavailable in builds that don't have ICU4X. Adapters using the browser-native `Intl` backend (`ars-i18n/web-intl`) cannot also use Highlight in the same build — the two ars-i18n backends are mutually exclusive.

+**`Chunk` is a parametric anatomy slot.** It is intentionally absent from the `Part` enum because its attribute output depends on a runtime boolean (`highlighted`) that `ConnectApi::part_attrs` cannot carry. Adapters call `Api::chunk_attrs(chunk.highlighted)` per chunk. The same shape applies to other parametric anatomies in the catalog (per-row, per-item, per-tag slots).
Adapters render each `HighlightChunk` as a `<mark>` (when `highlighted == true`) or `<span>` (when `highlighted == false`).
-- `<mark>` elements are used for highlighted text. Screen readers announce `<mark>` content as "highlighted" in most browser/AT combinations.
-- No ARIA attributes needed — the semantic `<mark>` element conveys the highlighting.
-- For custom styling, adapters set `data-ars-part="highlight-chunk"` and `data-ars-highlighted="true|false"` on each chunk.

-- When using fuzzy matching (`MatchStrategy::Fuzzy`), many small `<mark>` elements may be generated. Each `<mark>` is announced as 'highlighted' by screen readers, which can create excessive verbosity. For fuzzy matches with many small highlights, adapters SHOULD consolidate adjacent highlighted chunks into a single `<mark>` element, or provide a summary announcement (e.g., 'N characters matched') instead of wrapping each character individually.

+- `<mark>` is the semantic HTML element for highlighted text. Screen-reader announcement of `<mark>` content varies by browser/AT combination — VoiceOver and JAWS announce highlighted runs in many configurations; NVDA in its default profile does not. The agnostic core does not add ARIA attributes; adapters that need a guaranteed announcement (e.g., live-region tickers around the highlighted region) layer that on top.
+- For custom styling, adapters carry the `data-ars-part="highlight-chunk"` and `data-ars-highlighted="true|false"` attributes via `Api::chunk_attrs(highlighted)`.

+- **Adjacent / overlapping chunk consolidation is done in the agnostic core.** `highlight_chunks` merges overlapping and adjacent ranges before returning, so adapters never see back-to-back `<mark>` elements covering contiguous text and screen readers never get a stutter of "highlighted, highlighted, highlighted" for adjacent fuzzy hits. Adapters MAY still provide a summary announcement (e.g., "N characters matched") for high-cardinality fuzzy results, as a layered enhancement.

-When highlighting text in mixed-direction content (e.g., English query in Arabic text), chunk boundaries may disrupt BiDi ordering. The adapter SHOULD set `dir="auto"` on the root container element, and consider applying `unicode-bidi: isolate` on individual `<mark>` and `<span>` elements to prevent chunk boundaries from affecting text reordering.

+`Api::root_attrs()` emits `dir="auto"` on the root `<span>` so the browser picks the correct BiDi paragraph direction from the first strong character of the highlighted text — adapters do not need to add it themselves. Adapters MAY additionally apply `unicode-bidi: isolate` CSS to chunk elements when rendering English query terms inside Arabic or Hebrew text, to prevent chunk boundaries from re-ordering visually. The styling is a CSS concern outside the agnostic core's reach.
+Adapters spread `Api::root_attrs()` and `Api::chunk_attrs(highlighted)` rather than hardcoding the `data-ars-*` strings — keeping the attribute names typed and DRY against renames.
-- **Overall:** Full parity -- ars-ui is a superset.
-- **Divergences:** ars-ui uses `MatchStrategy` enum (Contains/StartsWith/Fuzzy) while Ark uses `exactMatch`/`matchAll` booleans. ars-ui adds locale-aware Unicode case folding and fuzzy matching.
+- **Overall:** Full parity — ars-ui is a superset.

+- **Divergences:** ars-ui uses `MatchStrategy` enum (Contains/StartsWith/Fuzzy) while Ark uses `exactMatch`/`matchAll` booleans. ars-ui adds locale-aware Unicode case folding, fuzzy matching, core-side adjacency merging, and a BiDi-safe root via `dir="auto"`.
